skill.name: skill-builder
skill.description: Create Claude Code Skills with valid YAML frontmatter, progressive disclosure structure, examples, and organized supporting files. Use it when building personal or project skills for Claude, Codex, or Claude Code workflows.
skill.summary: Build well-structured Claude Code Skills with templates, validation guidance, and publishing-ready organization.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 232 potential issues, but most are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, file names, and documentation links. The real risk is moderate: the skill teaches agents to create persistent Claude skill files under user or project directories, which is expected for this skill but should remain visible to users.

medium_findings.title: Documented Writes to Claude Skill Directories
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to create personal or project skill directories under ~/.claude/skills/ and .claude/skills/. This is legitimate for a skill-builder, but it can add persistent instructions that future Claude sessions may load.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem paths and file creation commands are explicit and match the skill purpose. The concern is controlled persistence, not hidden malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Skill Authoring Guide
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences and inline command examples as shell or Ruby execution. These are documentation examples for creating skill folders, SKILL.md files, and optional scripts, not executable files bundled with the skill. The examples can still cause local file writes if an agent follows them without user review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown examples rather than standalone executable scripts. Risk remains because agents may run these examples to create or modify local files during skill creation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to example domains, Anthropic documentation, GitHub repositories, and community links. No evidence found that the skill sends data to these URLs or performs automatic network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as Markdown links in resource sections. I did not find fetch, curl, webhook, or data upload behavior tied to these references.

low_findings.title: Relative Parent Paths Are Navigation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported path traversal sequences are Markdown links to related skills using ../ paths. They are not used in file access code, copy commands, or archive extraction logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a related-skills list inside a Markdown template. No evidence found of user-controlled path handling or filesystem traversal.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Markdown Name Collisions
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts correspond to text such as SKILL.md, README.md, Markdown examples, or descriptive content. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, hashing APIs, encryption code, or security-sensitive cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is documentation-heavy and the flagged contexts are skill metadata or Markdown references. No cryptographic operation appears in the reviewed context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples in Skill Authoring Guide
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences and inline command examples as shell or Ruby execution. These are documentation examples for creating skill folders, SKILL.md files, and optional scripts, not executable files bundled with the skill. The examples can still cause local file writes if an agent follows them without user review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown examples rather than standalone executable scripts. Risk remains because agents may run these examples to create or modify local files during skill creation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Writes to Claude Skill Directories
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to create personal or project skill directories under ~/.claude/skills/ and .claude/skills/. This is legitimate for a skill-builder, but it can add persistent instructions that future Claude sessions may load.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem paths and file creation commands are explicit and match the skill purpose. The concern is controlled persistence, not hidden malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Building Claude skills can fail when metadata, folders, or examples do not match the expected structure. This skill provides templates and validation guidance for creating reliable skills for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md YAML frontmatter fields and limits.
Shows personal and project skill directory layouts for Claude Code.
Provides basic, intermediate, and advanced skill templates.
Describes progressive disclosure for main instructions and referenced files.
Includes validation checklists for structure, content quality, and testing.
Gives examples for documentation and code-generation skills.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include executable helper scripts.
It does not automatically validate generated skills against a formal schema.
Some linked resources are examples and may need replacement in real skills.
It focuses on Claude Code skill structure, not every marketplace packaging format.

use_cases.title: Create a Personal Workflow Skill
use_cases.description: Build a local skill that captures repeated instructions, templates, and setup steps for personal productivity.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Skill Authoring
use_cases.description: Give a team a shared structure for project skills, examples, and supporting documentation.

use_cases.title: Prepare Marketplace Skill Content
use_cases.description: Draft a skill with clear metadata, progressive disclosure, validation notes, and user-facing examples.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic Claude Code skill named README Helper. Include valid frontmatter, a short overview, quick start steps, and a troubleshooting section.

prompt_templates.title: Add Skill Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete folder structure for a project skill that uses templates, examples, and documentation. Explain which files should stay in SKILL.md and which should be referenced.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill name and description for Claude Code discovery. Make the description specific, include clear trigger conditions, and keep it under the documented limit.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this draft skill for frontmatter validity, progressive disclosure, folder organization, runnable examples, and marketplace readiness. Return concrete fixes in priority order.

output_examples.input: Create a new skill for generating project READMEs.
output_examples.output: A structured skill draft with frontmatter, purpose, quick start instructions, usage steps, and troubleshooting guidance.

output_examples.input: Review my skill description for autonomous discovery.
output_examples.output: A clearer description that states what the skill does, when Claude should use it, and the main trigger phrases.

output_examples.input: Plan an advanced skill with scripts and resources.
output_examples.output: A recommended folder layout with SKILL.md, scripts, resources, documentation files, and notes on when each file is loaded.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused on core instructions and move long references into linked files.
Use specific trigger phrases in the description so Claude can select the skill reliably.
Review all filesystem commands before allowing an agent to create persistent skill files.
anti_patterns: Do not bury the main use case in a vague or overly broad description.
Do not place skills inside nested namespace folders that Claude Code will not discover.
Do not include scripts or links that users cannot inspect before execution.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create Claude Code Skills with valid metadata, organized instructions, templates, and supporting files.

faq.question: Can it create project and personal skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains both personal skills under the user Claude directory and project skills under a repository directory.

faq.question: Does it run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a documentation guide with command examples that users or agents may choose to run.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The guide includes commands that can create persistent Claude skill files, which is useful but needs user review.

faq.question: Does it support Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: The marketplace report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for skill-authoring workflows.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should verify frontmatter, directory placement, progressive disclosure, runnable examples, and any linked resources or scripts.
